recommendedChannel: watchdog-process-polling-plan-file plus separate-cli-confirmation-command plus TTY/manual fallback
minimumForNextBucket: separate CLI confirmation command or token file can confirm without app UI
automatic timeout restore works without visible display, mouse, cursor, or Hyprland keybinds
manual TTY restore command is printed before apply as a fallback, but is not the only recovery path

options.description: A CLI command loads a persisted plan and confirmation token, then records confirmed status before the deadline.
options.independentFromVisibleDisplay: partially; terminal or TTY access required
options.nextRequiredProof: spawn the watchdog CLI as a separate process against fixture paths and prove timeout restore if no confirmation command is run

options.description: The watchdog polls a confirmation file containing the expected token until timeout.
options.independentFromVisibleDisplay: partially; can be written from shell/TTY/script
options.nextRequiredProof: atomic token-file creation, stale-token rejection, and timeout restore in an independent process simulation

options.description: A background watchdog process owns the timeout and polls the persisted plan/result files.
options.nextRequiredProof: process lifetime, lock-file ownership, crash behavior, timeout restore, and result log handoff

options.description: An inert user-level service template documents how a future watchdog could be launched without root.
options.nextRequiredProof: inert template only now; later sprint must prove user-service start/stop without installing or requiring root

options.description: Before applying, print a command that restores from the backup and records a recovery result.
options.suitableForDebugCrash: partial; watchdog process still required for automatic crash recovery
options.nextRequiredProof: printed command correctness and backup path/token redaction policy

options.description: A final manual recovery fallback documents how to restore backup from a TTY if automated recovery fails.
options.nextRequiredProof: documented fallback; not sufficient alone for enablement because dead-man recovery must be automatic
